title: Sandbox strong-isolation tier: Tier-2 gVisor + Tier-3 Cloud Hypervisor microVM (Master Spec v02.187 §3.5)
goal: Implement the Master Spec v02.187 §3.5 isolation-tier model and strong-isolation adapters as real, working product code: the IsolationTier/TrustClass model and trust-keyed selection, a real Tier-3 CloudHypervisorAdapter (boots a Cloud Hypervisor microVM under WSL2+KVM, runs code, captures output), real read-write fs_bind, a real Tier-2 gVisor adapter, real microVM snapshot/restore, and the frontend capability type mirror. Folded into WP-KERNEL-004 per operator instruction (signature ilja290520260357).

host_dependencies.note: Strong-isolation tiers require Linux+KVM; on the Windows 11 host this is provided by WSL2. Real deps installed (operator-authorized): WSL2 Ubuntu, Cloud Hypervisor v52.0 + ch-remote, Firecracker-CI vmlinux-6.1.102, busybox initramfs, gVisor runsc release-20260525, /dev/kvm rw (wsl.conf boot hook + kvm group). Adapters fail-closed AdapterUnavailable on hosts lacking these.

scope.summary: Real implementation of the v02.187 §3.5 strong-isolation sandbox tier (no mocks): tier model + trust-keyed selection + CloudHypervisor Tier-3 microVM + fs_bind + gVisor Tier-2 + snapshot/restore + frontend type.

acceptance_criteria.requirement: IsolationTier{Tier1Container,Tier2Syscall,Tier3Microvm} + TrustClass{Trusted,Reviewed,UntrustedAgent default} + min_isolation_tier mapping; AdapterCapabilities gains isolation_tier/requires_nested_virt/supports_snapshot; ProcessSpec gains trust_class.
Real CloudHypervisorAdapter (Tier3Microvm) boots a Cloud Hypervisor microVM under WSL2+KVM and runs code in-guest, capturing real stdout + exit code; ephemeral microVM per exec.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: cloud_hypervisor_adapter_tests: real microVM stdout handshake-microvm-ok + Linux, rc=0; nonzero rc=7

acceptance_criteria.requirement: Trust-keyed selection routes UntrustedAgent to the weakest available tier >= Tier-3 (microVM), keeps Trusted/Reviewed on Tier-1, and fails closed (IsolationTierUnsatisfied) when no satisfying adapter exists. Never downgrades.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: sandbox_selection_tests: untrusted->Tier3 routing + trusted->Tier1 + fail-closed cases

acceptance_criteria.requirement: Real read-write fs_bind: host workspace dir baked into the microVM (initramfs-bake) and changes written back to the host via serial-tar. Reserved-path rejection.
Real Tier-2 GvisorAdapter runs commands in a gVisor (runsc) syscall sandbox via WSL2; registered; never the silent default.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: gvisor_adapter_tests: real sandbox gvisor-tier2-ok + Linux rc=0; nonzero rc=7

acceptance_criteria.requirement: Real CH microVM snapshot/restore (persistent-VM mode + ch-remote pause/snapshot + cloud-hypervisor --restore) preserves running in-RAM state across restore; trait gains snapshot/restore (default Unsupported); supports_snapshot=true.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: cloud_hypervisor_snapshot_restore_preserves_running_state PASS: in-RAM TICK counter continued across restore (before=1 -> after=4) = resumed from captured memory, not rebooted. SandboxAdapter gains default snapshot/restore (typed SnapshotUnsupported); LedgerDecorator delegates snapshot + records a restore START; SnapshotRef.observe_path reattaches the restored serial stream. cloud_hypervisor_adapter_tests 5/5 green.

acceptance_criteria.requirement: Frontend AdapterCapabilities TS type mirrors the new fields + IsolationTier.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: app/src/components/diagnostics/sandbox/types.ts (additive; full pnpm build not run in this env)

validator_focus: Confirm the strong-isolation adapters genuinely boot real sandboxes (not mocks) where WSL2+KVM is available; confirm AdapterUnavailable fail-closed where not.
Confirm the trust->tier selection cannot place untrusted-agent code on a Tier-1 shared-kernel adapter.
Confirm fs_bind write-back cannot escape the declared bind path; reserved guest paths rejected.
Confirm snapshot/restore preserves state and that exec-into-persistent-VM (vsock agent) is explicitly deferred, not faked.
risk_if_missed: Without strong isolation, untrusted agent-written code runs in shared-kernel containers (host-compromise on escape). Without trust-keyed routing, the tier model is advisory only.
known_followups: exec-into-persistent-VM via a vsock guest agent (snapshot/restore VMs are currently snapshot-only, not exec-into).
virtio-fs/disk live fs_bind requires a virtio-fs/virtio-pci-capable guest kernel (current Firecracker kernel lacks them; initramfs-bake is used).
reconcile the older kernel/sandbox MicroVmAdapterStub (MT-018/020 HardIsolationAdapter layer) with the new sandbox/ Tier-3 CloudHypervisorAdapter.
frontend: run pnpm build/tests + add diagnostics table rows for the new capability fields.
persist /dev/kvm permissions verified across a full WSL shutdown cycle.

risks: A 'real' adapter could secretly mock execution; mitigated by integration tests that boot actual microVMs/sandboxes and assert in-guest output.
Trust-tier guard could be bypassed if selection picks the default adapter without re-checking tier; covered by the routing fix + end-to-end test.
fs_bind write-back could overwrite host paths outside the bind; mitigated by per-bind guest-path validation + bind-scoped copy-back.
minimum_controls: Every strong-isolation adapter has a real integration test that boots an actual sandbox and asserts real in-guest output.
Selection fails closed (typed IsolationTierUnsatisfied) rather than downgrading untrusted workloads.
Adapters declare runtime_available only when the real runtime is present (try_new probe).
